level_signals: 5-8 years in RevOps, sales operations or sales finance in a B2B environment
Owns and administers GTM variable compensation architecture end-to-end
Serves as RevOps lead on post-close M&A integration for GTM
Reports to Head of Revenue Operations
US base range $115,000–$240,000 indicates wide band covering mid-to-senior levels

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have 5-8 years in RevOps, sales operations or sales finance in a B2B environment
Hands on experience designing and administering variable compensation plans (not just reporting on them)
Experience supporting or leading GTM workstreams in M&A integrations
Have financial modelling fluency, building bottoms up capacity models, compensation scenarios, and attainment distribution
Are a clear communicator able to present complex compensation plans or compensation frameworks to sales leadership and executives
Are comfortable working in ambiguous, fast moving environments

scope.surface: GTM variable compensation architecture and post-close M&A GTM integration
primary_metrics_inferred: OTE attainment rates across GTM teams
core_responsibilities: Own and administer GTM variable compensation architecture including accelerators and SPIFs across sales, BDRs, CSMs, SAs and the partner team
Model scenarios to stress test costs, attainment distributions, and behavioural incentives before plans are finalized
Partner with finance on commission actuals and with HR on benchmarking against market data

Own the plan roll out with the people team and GTM leadership including documentation, communications, resolution frameworks, exception handling/tracking, system configuration and compensation governance
Work with sales enablement to ensure reps have a clear path to OTE - identifying quota, ramp, and efficiency gaps before they impact attainment
Partner with finance and GTM leadership to model headcount unlock criteria, identifying when and where to hire to support growth in new regions and segments

Serve as the RevOps lead on all post-close GTM integration items, including designing and maintaining an integration playbook covering role mapping, territory carving, quota assignment, system integration items and data mapping
Track integration milestones and surface risks to RevOps leadership and cross-functional integration teams

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and is wide ($115K–$240K USD base), indicating flexibility for strong candidates
Role has clear ownership and scope — 'design and execution role' with explicit accountability
Reports to Head of Revenue Operations — direct access to senior leadership
Remote US eligible despite office locations listed
Generous benefits: 6 weeks PTO, 401K, full health and dental, parental leave top-up
Encourages applicants whose experience doesn't line up exactly
red_flags: Very wide compensation band ($115K–$240K) may indicate ambiguity in level/seniority being targeted
Location section says 'Remote US' but qualifications section lists 'NYC, SF, Toronto' — mild inconsistency worth confirming
No explicit mention of equity despite being a high-growth AI company — may be an omission
M&A integration experience is a hard requirement that is relatively rare and non-bridgeable
Role combines two distinct disciplines (comp design + M&A integration) which could mean high scope/breadth demands
